commit.message:
Add create task dialog, auto-save, multi-file drop, and comments/data fixes

- Add CreateTaskDialog: when filters are active, Enter opens a dialog instead
  of inserting an inline block, supporting #hashtag and @mention parsing
- Add debounced auto-save (3s default) for inline note title edits
- Support multi-file drag & drop in BlockNoteEditor with parallel uploads,
  and inject delete buttons on file blocks
- Simplify comments UI: remove reply/resolve threading, flatten to a linear
  comment list with compact layout and toast on save
- Make comments fully optimistic: instant UI updates with pending-mutation
  guard to prevent realtime subscription from overwriting local state
- Fix foreign key violations: await note insert in Supabase before emitting
  label/assignee events in createNote and createNoteAfter
- Add optimistic assignee cache updates via event subscription
- Change default note sort from sort_order to created_at descending
- Fix GCal calendar fetch deduplication with one-shot ref
- Add is_default field to Contact type, fix Supabase type casts
- Remove note_comments updated_at DB trigger (managed in app code)
- Add i18n keys for create task dialog and comment saved toast (en/es/pt)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct api test mocks for jsdom environment and flexible header matching

Use jsdom's native window instead of stubbing globalThis, and match headers
with objectContaining to avoid brittleness from additional default headers.

docs: improve English translation quality and conventions

Page titles updated to follow standard English tech doc conventions:
- Configuration Parameters → Configuration Reference
- URL Format → URL Formats
- Time Processing Specification → Time Processing
- Performance Benchmark Report → Performance Benchmark
- Public Access Recommendations → Public Access Guide

Terminology fixes:
- "compile" → "build" throughout installation.md
- "OpenWrt backend" → "OpenWrt admin panel"
- Remove Chinese characters from English docs (万事大吉, 央视)

Meaning fidelity fixes:
- Add "Total" to CPU usage stat (25 streams combined, not per-stream)
- "high-performance x86 devices" preserving original specificity

Grammar and naturalness improvements:
- Feature descriptions use consistent grammatical structure
- Restructured awkward phrasings in video-snapshot intro
- Cross-reference link texts updated to match current page titles

commit.message:
Initial commit: add gitignore and architecture spec

Set up project with .gitignore and the PrivacyGuard Technical Architecture Spec PDF as the foundational reference document.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix config drops, export composable APIs, remove dead patterns (#182)

* Fix phail-forge missing llm config, export batch utilities and missing modules

phail-forge hardcoded the llm import and accepted no model config, so
consumers couldn't control which model was used. Renamed import to
callLlm and added llm + ...restOptions forwarding to both calls.

central-tendency-lines destructured config without ...options spread,
silently dropping any extra params consumers passed (e.g. abortSignal).

Exported missing modules from shared.js:
- centralTendencyLines verblet (single-item centrality evaluation)
- extractFeatures chain (parallel multi-feature extraction)
- createBatches, parallel, normalizeLlm, batchTracker (batch utilities
  for consumers building custom chains)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Export all instruction builders and spec functions, fix READMEs

Exported ~40 composable functions from shared.js that were previously
only accessible via internal chain paths: entitySpec, relationSpec,
tagSpec, scaleSpec, anonymizeSpec, applyScore, scoreItem, and all
{map,filter,reduce,find,group}Instructions for score, entities,
relations, tags, scale, and anonymize chains. Also exported applyEntities,
applyRelations, applyTags, applyScale, applyAnonymization and their
factory functions (createEntityExtractor, createRelationExtractor, etc).

Fixed verblets README: removed duplicate name entry, removed wrong
date/to-object references (chains not verblets), added 5 missing
verblets (central-tendency-lines, commonalities, expect, fill-missing,
phail-forge).

Fixed chains README: added extract-blocks and extract-features.

Fixed DESIGN.md: replaced non-existent codeQuality example, fixed
incorrect expect API pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix map README referencing non-existent listMapLines verblet

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix generateList shouldStop receiving empty arrays instead of actual results

The per-query shouldStop callback at the end of each generator loop
received hardcoded empty arrays for resultsAll and resultsNew, making
it impossible for consumers to implement stop conditions based on
collected results. The per-result shouldStop worked correctly but the
per-query check (which runs when all results in a batch were skipped)
always saw zero results.

Also updated list mock to return parsed objects (matching structured
output behavior) instead of raw JSON strings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove redundant typeof string JSON.parse checks

callLlm with response_format already parses JSON and returns objects.
The typeof === 'string' ? JSON.parse : identity pattern was dead code
that obscured the actual contract. Removed from list, detect-threshold,
intersections, test-analysis, and DESIGN.md examples.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Remove modelService import from list chain, use DEFAULT_LIST_TIMEOUT_MS constant
- Remove modelService import from scan-js, thread llm param from consumer
- Remove modelService import from category-samples, pass llm directly to list()
- Add debug logging to silent catch blocks in find and group chains

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- disambiguate: forward maxAttempts to score() call
- timeline: forward maxAttempts to reduce() and map() enrichment calls
- scan-js: remove modelName from modelOptions (handled by llm param)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- group: forward now to reduce() category discovery call
- entities: forward now to entitySpec() and applyEntities() calls
- Export calculateStatistics from detect-threshold
- Export generateList generator from list chain
- Export listBatch, ListStyle, determineStyle from list-batch

- Export scientificFramingPrompt, causalFramePrompt, softCoverPrompt from veiled-variants

  generateWithRetry only throws retriable errors.
- Remove unsupported now/chainStartTime params from retry() calls in

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove redundant onlyJSON prompt constants, add missing retry labels

Remove onlyJSON/onlyJSONStringArray from prompts in 10 files where
response_format with json_schema already constrains the output format.
The prompt constants were redundant — structured output makes the model
return valid JSON matching the schema without prompt instructions.

Files cleaned: entities, disambiguate, tags, relations, scale, score,
pop-reference, tag-vocabulary, central-tendency-lines, commonalities.

Also:

- Export progress callback utilities from shared.js: emitProgress,

- Export windowFor from shared.js for sliding window text processing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

When response_format with json_schema is set, prompt instructions like
asJSON, asWrappedArrayJSON, and asWrappedValueJSON are redundant since
the LLM is already constrained to output valid JSON matching the schema.

Cleaned 12 files: date, dismantle, intersections, questions, test,
bool, enum, fill-missing, name, name-similar-to, number, sentiment.

Also: fix onProgress/maxAttempts threading in scan-js and test-analyzer,
fix config rest-spread in questions chain, add missing exports to
shared.js (parallelMap, embed-neighbor-chunks utilities, progress
callback lifecycle functions).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove 19 dead prompt constants from reflective/analytical sections

These constants (identifyUnclearInfo, argueAgainstOutput, rateBasic,
rateSatisfaction, rewriteBasedOnRating, summarizeRequest,
considerProsCons, provideExamples, explainReasoning,
alternativeSolutions, explainToChild, identifyAssumptions,
alternativeInterpretations, evidenceSupportsView,
limitationsOfApproach, missingInformation, evaluateDifferingViews,
confidenceInResponse, lessKnowledgeResponse, analogyForUnderstanding)
were never used in any chain, verblet, or prompt template.

Kept expertResponse which is used in generate-questions.js.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add spec tests for detect-threshold, category-samples, list-batch; clean up index.js

New specs (120 tests total):
- detect-threshold: 45 tests covering calculateStatistics, input validation,
  reduce/callLlm delegation, config forwarding, out-of-range filtering
- category-samples: 40 tests covering basic call, llm/retry/onProgress
  forwarding, error handling, buildSeedGenerationPrompt, categorySamplesList
- list-batch: 35 tests covering basic call, llm forwarding, list styles,
  determineStyle, edge cases

Also removed 7 redundant RAG export lines from index.js — these are
already exported from shared.js via the embed-expand-query chain barrel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix reversed asXML arguments in detect-threshold, add intersections spec

detect-threshold had asXML('tagName', content) instead of
asXML(content, { tag: 'tagName' }) — goal text, statistics, and
percentiles were silently lost from prompts (only the tag name string
appeared as content).

New spec: intersections chain (44 tests) covering combination
generation, llm/retry/onProgress forwarding, batch processing,
response_format, minSize/maxSize, useSchemaValidation path, error
handling, and restOptions threading.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add AbortSignal support to retry utility

Consumers building real applications need cancellation. retry() now
accepts an abortSignal option that checks before each attempt and
makes sleep cancellable via signal listener.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Thread abortSignal from consumer config to all retry calls

Every chain that calls retry() now forwards abortSignal, enabling
consumers to cancel operations during retry sleep between attempts.
Also fixes score chain missing onProgress in retry calls.

34 files changed across all chains: anonymize, category-samples, date,
detect-threshold, disambiguate, dismantle, entities, extract-blocks,
filter, find, group, intersections, join, list, map, people,
pop-reference, questions, reduce, relations, scale, scan-js, score,
set-interval, socratic, sort, split, tag-vocabulary, tags,
test-analyzer, test, timeline, to-object, veiled-variants.

* Export anySignal, TimedAbortController, and templateReplace from shared.js

Consumer utilities for abort signal composition, request timeouts,
and template string replacement — complements the new abortSignal
support across all chains.

Fix logo transparency and section-solution background on iOS

  fully removes the logo's white background
- Replace section-solution background-attachment:fixed (broken on iOS)
  with a position:absolute pseudo-element at opacity 0.22

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix sphere boundary dye oscillation via backtrace clamp attenuation

When edge pixels have outward velocity, their advection backtrace goes
outside the sphere and gets clamped to the boundary surface. Multiple
pixels sample the same boundary point, duplicating dye. Combined with
velocity reflection at the boundary, this creates a periodic brightness
oscillation (~4s cycle at simSpeed 3).

Fix: attenuate dye proportionally to how far the backtrace overshot the
sphere. The further outside it went, the more the sampled value is faded,
preventing the duplication from accumulating.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add 33 LaunchWeek guides across 6 days (Days 0-5)

CMO content deliverable for DAT-239. Complete marketing playbook for
launching a SaaS in 5 days — research, positioning, content, distribution,
conversion, and launch execution.

- Day 0: Research (5 guides) — market research, ICP, positioning, validation
- Day 1: Position (5 guides) — value prop, pricing, landing page, brand voice, pitch
- Day 2: Content (6 guides) — SEO, blog posts, social, email, repurposing, AEO/GEO
- Day 3: Distribute (5 guides) — social profiles, community, outreach, partnerships, GitHub
- Day 4: Convert (6 guides) — analytics, A/B testing, onboarding, pricing page, Stripe, churn
- Day 5: Launch (6 guides) — checklist, community launch, HN, press, announcements, retrospective

FastWrite featured as primary Data Advantage product throughout.
Cross-links to VibeWeek, LLMReference, MetricGen, Pixola, DeckChat, STORYD.

commit.message:
feat(eventos): banner de bloqueo adaptado a plataforma mobile

Lógica por plataforma (si no se detecta bloqueador activo):
- iOS → Brave Browser (bloqueador integrado, App Store)
- Android + no Firefox → Firefox para Android (Play Store) + nota para instalar uBlock
- Android + Firefox → instalar uBlock Origin en Firefox (Add-ons)
- Desktop Firefox → uBlock en Firefox Add-ons
- Desktop Chrome/Edge → uBlock en Chrome Web Store

La detección (adsbox offsetHeight=0) aplica igual en todos los casos:
si Brave/Firefox ya tiene bloqueo activo, no se muestra el banner.

commit.message:
fix: reconnect Matrix after build to avoid stale TCP connections

bootstrapBot blocks the event loop for 60-80s (npm ci + build),
which kills all TCP connections. Post-build thread replies fail with
"fetch failed: other side closed" because undici reuses dead connections.

Fix: call connectRoom(conn) immediately after build to get a fresh
Matrix session before sending stage updates and the checklist.

fix(resilience): auth CB NameError fix, idem clear key, read-only slug multi-word prefix, pagination cap, PG CB cache, result cache

- fix: _mark_slug_failed NameError → _record_slug_failure (auth CB was fully broken on 401 path)
- add: _clear_idem_key() to remove idempotency key after tool failure (allow legitimate retry)
- fix: _is_read_only_slug() now handles multi-word service prefixes (MICROSOFT_TEAMS_SEARCH_*)
- feat: pagination hard cap at 100 pages — prevents runaway slug index loop
- feat: PG CB cache (_pg_cb_cache, 60s TTL) — eliminates per-tool-call PG round-trip for cross-worker CB checks
- feat: tool result cache (_tool_result_cache) for read-only slugs with per-prefix TTLs (30–120s)
- feat: idempotency keys (_executed_call_keys, 5min TTL) — prevents duplicate writes during parallel_tool_calls + heartbeat storms

feat(color): add WCAG contrast ratio checker (#70)

* feat(color): add WCAG contrast ratio checker

Extends the Color tool with a live WCAG contrast checker:

that mirrors the main color picker. Users now see both the foreground
and background colors together, making the linkage explicit.

- Read-only swatch (dashed border) + hex output auto-updated in updateContrast()

- Zero new interactive elements; main picker remains single source of truth

Closes #73

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(hodl-waves): daily-only classification + 5x batch size

- _process_block(classify=False): skip O(pool_size) band classification
  for non-boundary blocks — called every block, 313k entries = expensive
- Daily classification: emit 1 row per UTC day (first block of new day)
  instead of 1 row per block — 144x fewer classification calls
- Batch size: 2016 → 10080 blocks (5x fewer JSON pool saves, ~6MB each)
- Flush trigger: block-count based, not row-count based
- Pool update (spending inputs + adding outputs) still runs every block

Expected speedup: ~1.5-2x — classification was ~30-40% of CPU at h>400k.

commit.message:
Add GitHub repo filter plugin for allowlist/blocklist enforcement (#139)

## Summary

This PR introduces a new `github-repo-filter` plugin that enforces
repository-level access control on GitHub MCP tool calls. The plugin
supports both allowlist and blocklist modes with fnmatch-style pattern
matching, and can be scoped to specific MCP servers.

## Key Changes

- **New plugin module** (`src/mcp_zero/plugins/github_repo_filter.py`):
- `GitHubRepoFilterHook`: Lifecycle hook that intercepts tool calls at
POST_VALIDATION stage (priority 55)
- `GitHubRepoFilterPlugin`: Plugin class that validates configuration
and registers the hook
- Extracts `owner` and `repo` from tool arguments and enforces filtering
rules
  - Supports optional server-name scoping to apply filtering selectively

- **Comprehensive test suite**
(`tests/plugins/test_github_repo_filter.py`):
  - Plugin lifecycle tests (configuration validation, hook registration)
- Hook behavior tests covering allowlist/blocklist modes, pattern
matching, case-insensitivity, and server scoping
  - Edge cases like non-repo-scoped tools and missing arguments

- **Entry point registration** (`pyproject.toml`):
  - Added `github-repo-filter` entry point for plugin discovery

## Implementation Details

- **Hook positioning**: Runs after governance checks (priority 55 vs 50)
to provide fine-grained repo-level filtering on top of broader access
policies
- **Pattern matching**: Uses Python's `fnmatch` with case-insensitive
comparison (e.g., `myorg/*` matches all repos in an organization)
- **Configuration validation**: Enforces mutually exclusive modes,
non-empty repo lists, and proper `owner/repo` format
- **Server scoping**: Optional `servers` config list restricts filtering
to specific MCP server names; omitting it applies filtering to all
servers
- **Pass-through behavior**: Non-repo-scoped tools (those without
`owner`/`repo` arguments) are allowed through unchanged

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ShBDggHqZU3DZLJdDufNqU

commit.message:
Switch from HashRouter to BrowserRouter for clean URLs

- Replace HashRouter with BrowserRouter, removes /#/ from all URLs
- Update link hrefs from #/ to /
- Add 404.html redirect for GitHub Pages SPA support
- Add path restore script in index.html

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 10-agent LangGraph pipeline, TinyDB storage, and token-aware credential persistence

- agents/: 10 parallel agents (fundamental, technical, dcf, sentiment, hot_stocks,
  portfolio, macro_risk, alerts, comparative, trend) + cost_agent, market_opinion,
  final_summary, evaluator; each with deterministic input/output contracts,
  per-node quality eval, and exponential-backoff retry on API failures
- analysis.py: thin LangGraph orchestrator; dynamic graph built from enabled agents
- market_data.py: comprehensive yfinance fetch (ETF-aware, institutional, insider,
  calendar, benchmark comparison, portfolio metrics, rule-based alerts)
- db.py: TinyDB store with S3-style blob files; stores RH session pickle (base64)
  and Anthropic key so credentials.py is only needed on first run
- broker_robinhood.py: token-aware login — restores session pickle from DB before
  login and backs up the updated pickle after, avoiding password round-trips
- config.py: load_credentials() tries DB first, falls back to credentials.py and
  auto-saves to DB for future runs
- analyze.py: --user, --config, --migrate flags; UTF-8 stdout fix for Windows;
  passes DB to broker for token persistence
- run_config.py + analysis.toml: TOML config for all 10 agents, budget, reports
- validation.py: pre-run checks (API key, user budget, budget floor, snapshot exists)
- Remove data_store.py (legacy gzip store, migration complete)
- .gitignore: exclude portfolio_db.json, data/, robinhood.pickle, credentials.py
- README.md + AGENTS.md: full documentation and flow diagram

- Arrow keys now only change direction when pressing perpendicular to current direction (no longer moves to adjacent cell on direction change)

commit.message:
Exclude sub-05 ses-02 truncated tone scan from conversion

Tone scan had 279/320 volumes (truncated). Sub-05 has 2 good tone runs
in ses-03, so the ses-02 run is excluded from BIDS entirely. Removed
tone task from overrides.toml and ses-02_conf.json; files deleted from
BIDS root and derivatives (mriqc, fmriprep, fmriprep_pre022426).

repository.name: mmmdata
repository.description: Multi-Modal Memory Dataset

commit.message:
fix: Add curl to Dockerfile and improve healthcheck configuration

- Add curl to apk packages for healthcheck support
- Switch from wget to curl for healthcheck command
- Increase start-period from 5s to 30s to allow services time to initialize
- Increase retries from 3 to 5 for better resilience

This fixes the 'curl or wget command' warning in Coolify and ensures
the healthcheck has enough time for PostgreSQL and other dependencies
to become available before the backend starts responding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(mobile): agent chat keyboard positioning on edge-to-edge Android

Replace KeyboardAvoidingView with manual keyboard height tracking.
Uses screen.height - screenY for accurate measurement. Input bar
sits above tab bar when closed, just above keyboard when open.
Background set to transparent for global gradient pass-through.

commit.message:
Add .gitattributes to mark PBOs and other binaries as binary

Prevents git autocrlf from corrupting binary files on checkout,
which was causing EnableInventoryInVehicle.pbo to appear modified
and blocking git pull on client machines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix numpy array handling in Keepa service

The keepa SDK returns numpy arrays for history data, which crash on
boolean evaluation (`if not array:` → "ambiguous truth value"). Fixed by:
- Converting numpy arrays to Python lists via `_get_array()` helper
- Replacing `_is_nan()` with `_is_valid()` using `math.isnan(float(v))`
- Explicit `float()` casts when extracting values from numpy arrays
- Using `len()` checks instead of boolean evaluation for arrays

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Sprint K-1: auth hardening, RLS fixes, PHI scrubbing, infra hardening

- GAP-01/02/03/16: Auth guards on all /api/admin/*, /api/ai/*, case mutations, demo-credentials
- GAP-04/07/08/09/10/20: RLS policies, missing tables, audit_log, unskipped tests
- GAP-05/11/18/19: scrubPrompt(), scrubOutput(), injection detection, PHI boundary in case detail
- GAP-06/17: Remove PHI from extended_profile, portal profile uses patient-scoped client
- GAP-12: Security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options) in next.config.ts + vercel.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Closes #80 — Scaffold Pillar mesh (#339)

commit.message:
feat: Support go-to-definition for keyword/rest/optional parameters

Add handling for required keyword, optional keyword, keyword rest,
optional, and rest parameter nodes in local variable definition finder.
Use name_loc() instead of location() for parameters with prefix symbols
(*, **) to point to the name rather than the operator.

Block parameters (&block) are not yet supported due to how ruby-prism
parses the reference.
